role: Generated cross-language client boundary for Discovery producer contracts

integrations.notes: All three generated clients implement exact Data Engine discovery-release commit and get operations. Canonical source locks bind merged Data Engine main head 6c889513d550e2c8fab1c35ac9f8cd667f90703e.

Producer-derived route metadata selects the tempera-bio human bearer or tempera-workflows service bearer for signer, approval, and provider-resolution operations. Canonical provenance binds merged Auth Hub main head 1c0c4b9baf7dd49b755432fe14c66e6d7ff0292f.

The canonical source lock and generated methods bind merged Gym main head b5d8369824cfdeae1a0cbbeb882af6fa19258cb2 for task, trainer, episode, and sealed-evaluation contracts; no external sealed result is claimed.

All three generated clients expose deriveCampaignState with the exact lowerCamel wire body and bio:decision:write scope. Canonical provenance binds merged Bio main head 35f3d5d2c1cf7025e5b7795a7a4a2728a6743cd8; the SDK transports replay inputs but does not derive biology, run a campaign, or claim physical evidence.

All three generated clients expose runs.signal with the exact run path, digest-bound callback body, and literal custom-method colon. Canonical provenance binds current Workflows main head f83acfd0653cd11c4ead536ddc315bd586b7e2fc, whose committed OpenAPI blob is unchanged; no hosted callback or external experiment result is claimed.

limitations: Generated Discovery methods prove cross-language dispatch and contract conformance, not a hosted production call.
Canonical source locks prove exact contract provenance and generated-client parity; they do not prove that any hosted producer deployment has adopted those commits.
Durable-signal client tests prove request construction and exact producer-contract parity; they do not prove receipt of a real provider callback or successful external execution.
Authorization clients transport approvals and external secret references; they do not approve, resolve a secret, submit physical work, or prove provider execution without an authorized caller.
The SDK transports evidence contracts; it does not determine biological validity, experimental execution, production qualification, or dataset eligibility.
